Additionally, thyroglossal cysts do not have such specific movements related to tongue manipulation; they usually present as midline neck masses without direct correlation to lingual activity.

Answer: C Your agreement aligns well with the provided rationale regarding the unique characteristic of a ranula moving during tongue protrusion.
